title: ai-experiments
description: A quick prototyping platform for exploring LLM internals — residual streams, attention patterns, and hidden states. Built to run fast experiments and share discoveries.

title: ComfyUI Tools
description: A collection of custom nodes and tools for ComfyUI, the node-based Stable Diffusion GUI. Includes a login/auth system, an auto-updating leaderboard of the top 100 ComfyUI repos, a B-LoRA loader for style-content separation, a RetinaFace-based face cropper, and a Jupyter-style Python execution node (the precursor to ai-experiments).

title: gym-codecraft
description: A gym-like environment with a Docker container sandbox for an RL agent to learn to write code.

title: UE5 MassEntity Demo
description: A tutorial and demo showing how to use the experimental MassEntity system in Unreal Engine 5 to create 1,000 independently moving cubes with high performance.

title: Voxcraft-sim
description: A voxel-based physics simulation for evolutionary robotics, implemented in CUDA. I rewrote a CPU-only simulator to run on multiple GPUs, enabling much larger experiments in soft-robot evolution.

title: Xenobots
description: Living robots designed by computers, realized using frog cells in petri dishes. I was responsible for improving the computational simulation for the automated design of these biological machines.

title: Hinton's Course Demos
description: Interactive demos I built while taking Geoffrey Hinton's 'Neural Networks for Machine Learning' course. Plus a PyCUDA implementation of Jack's Car Rental from Sutton's RL book.
